name: Gyros (Doners, Yeros)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb with the bread, allspice, coriander, garlic, onion, savory, and salt and pepper. Knead thoroughly until the mixture is spicy but not too herby and holds its shape.
2. Break the mixture into 5 sections, each about the size of a navel orange. Divide each section into 6 balls.
3. Flatten each ball slightly to a thickness of about 3/4". Arrange bacon slices between the meat balls, keeping them separated.
4. Roll gently with your palms to smooth the edges around each bacon slice, creating 5 or 6 skewers total.
5. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
6. When ready to cook, place on a broiler tray or grill under moderate heat. Turn every 5 minutes; the bacon will baste the meat during cooking.
7. The meat should be crusty on top with fully cooked interior in about 25 minutes.
8. Serve pita pockets hot, topped with seasoned tomatoes, fresh parsley and yogurt, and any desired seasonings.
(Note: This recipe is based on "The Food of Greece" by Vilma Liacouras Chantiles.)
